Some TRUSTED_PROXIES entries are invalid: Some TRUSTED_PROXIES entries are invalid and were ignored.
Some game accounts are temporarily unava: Some game accounts are temporarily unavailable because their LoginServer has no configured GameServer. They remain safe and continue to count toward the account limit.

Synchronous is the simplest option. Asyn: Synchronous is the simplest option. Asynchronous responds faster. The database queue additionally preserves pending emails across restarts.
Synchronous mode is compatible with ever: Synchronous mode is compatible with every server. Background mode returns the page immediately and sends the email in a separate PHP process without a permanent queue worker.
Temporarily unavailable game accounts al: Temporarily unavailable game accounts also count toward the limit.

Use 3 to 40 lowercase Latin letters, num: Use 3 to 40 lowercase Latin letters, numbers, and single hyphens. Leave the suffix empty to use /admin.
Use TRUSTED_PROXIES=* only when the web: Use TRUSTED_PROXIES=* only when the web server cannot be reached directly.
Use a neutral technical icon and never i: Use a neutral technical icon and never inherit the profile avatar.
Use a separate CMS administrator account: Use a separate CMS administrator account. A game account cannot be used here.

Use only lowercase Latin letters, number: Use only lowercase Latin letters, numbers, and single hyphens.
Use recovery only when the original upda: Use recovery only when the original update request has stopped or failed to finish. A running update keeps an exclusive lock and cannot be recovered at the same time.
Use the login or email of your site acco: Use the login or email of your site account.
Use the restart action after changing SM: Use the restart action after changing SMTP or other settings loaded when a permanent worker starts.
